CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, content STRING);
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (1, 'hello world'), (2, 'duckdb is great'), (3, 'fuzzy testing'), (4, 'test contains function'), (5, 'abc'), (6, 'def');
SELECT id, content FROM test_strings WHERE contains(content, 'duck') = TRUE;
SELECT id, content FROM test_strings WHERE contains(content, 'test') AND id > 3;
SELECT id, content FROM test_strings WHERE contains(content, 'o') OR contains(content, 'f');
SELECT id, content FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE contains(content, 'abc'));
SELECT id, content FROM test_strings WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE contains(content, 'xyz'));
SELECT id, content FROM test_strings WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE contains(content, 'e')) > 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE contains(content, 'great') GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT id, content FROM test_strings WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_strings AS t2 WHERE contains(t2.content, 'testing') AND t2.id = test_strings.id);
SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_strings WHERE contains(content, 'l') AND id < 5;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (ref_id INT, description STRING);
INSERT INTO additional_data VALUES (1, 'example with duck'), (2, 'additional fun'), (3, 'another test');
SELECT t1.id, t1.content, t2.description FROM test_strings t1 JOIN additional_data t2 ON contains(t1.content, 'test') AND t1.id = t2.ref_id;
SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE length(content) > 10 AND contains(content, 'e') = TRUE;
SELECT content FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM additional_data WHERE description LIKE '%duck%') AND contains(content, 'test');
